title: Chelsea buns
subtitle: Spiced currant spiral buns with a sticky glaze
excerpt: Chelsea buns are made by spreading enriched dough with butter, brown sugar, currants, and mixed spice, rolling it into a cylinder, and slicing it into rounds. Baked touching in a square tin, they pull apart into sticky spirals glazed with golden syrup.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136 — currant-filled rolled buns appear in the bread chapter as a variant on enriched dough. The Chelsea bun originates from the Chelsea Bun House (eighteenth century London), documented widely in Victorian domestic cookery.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unglazed. Defrost at room temperature and warm in a low oven. Glaze before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Shape and prove overnight in the fridge. Bake from cold, adding 5 minutes to the bake time.

glossaryTerms.definition: A bun made by spreading filling on a sheet of dough, rolling it into a cylinder, then slicing crossways into rounds. Placed touching in a tin and baked, the buns merge together and pull apart along the cut edges, revealing the spiral of filling inside.

content.text: : enriched dough rolled into a sheet, spread with a spiced currant filling, wound into a cylinder, and sliced into rounds. The buns are placed cut-side up in the tin so the spiral faces upward. Baking them touching means the sides stay soft while the tops brown; the golden syrup glaze is applied the moment they come out of the oven.
Mix, knead, and bulk ferment, 1 hour

Combine {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{yeast-fast-action}}, {{salt-table}}, and {{caster-sugar}}. Add the {{whole-milk}} and {{unsalted-butter}} from the dough group. Mix to a soft dough and knead for 8 minutes until smooth. Cover and leave until doubled, about 1 hour.

Roll the dough into a rectangle roughly 30 × 40 cm. Spread the softened filling {{unsalted-butter}} across the surface, leaving a 2 cm border at one long edge. Scatter over the {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{currants}}, and {{mixed-spice}}. Roll the dough tightly from the filled long edge toward the bare border, keeping the roll even. Cut into 9–12 rounds about 3 cm thick. Line a 23–25 cm square tin with baking paper and arrange the rounds cut-side up, fitting snugly. Cover and prove for 30 minutes until puffed and touching. Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan).

Bake for 18–20 minutes until deep gold on top. While they bake, warm the {{golden-syrup}} briefly. Brush generously over the buns the moment they come out of the oven. Leave to cool in the tin for 10 minutes, then transfer to a rack. Pull apart to serve.
